qa_pairs.question: How can users explicitly control the initial and maximum heap size for the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) using command-line options?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can explicitly control the initial and maximum heap size for the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) using command-line options. This can be done with either `java -Xms256m -Xmx4g -version` (where `-Xms` sets the initial heap size and `-Xmx` sets the maximum heap size) or `java -XX:InitialHeapSize=256m -XX:MaxHeapSize=4g -version`.

qa_pairs.question: What command-line flag allows users to inspect all options that the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) will use during execution?
qa_pairs.answer: To see all the command-line options the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) is going to run with, users can specify the flag `-XX:+PrintCommandLineFlags`. An example of its usage is `java -Xms256m -Xmx4g -XX:+PrintCommandLineFlags -version`.
